title: How to File a Divorce Petition in Bangladesh: Step-by-Step Guide
metaTitle: File Divorce Fast: Top Family Lawyer in Dhaka
metaDescription: Thinking of divorce? Get discreet, fast, and urgent legal help from our experienced family lawyer in Uttara, Dhaka. Settle your disputes smoothly.
heroIntro: Filing a divorce petition in Bangladesh involves specific legal procedures that differ based on religion and the type of divorce sought. This guide explains the complete process — from documentation to court filing — so you can proceed with confidence.
toc: Divorce Options in Bangladesh: Which Applies to You?
Documents Required to File for Divorce
Filing in Family Court: Step by Step
Timeline: How Long Does Divorce Take?

sections.h2: Divorce Options in Bangladesh: Which Applies to You?
sections.content:
Your divorce procedure depends on your religion and marital circumstances: You should also consider the rules for transferring property ownership legally if joint assets exist.

  • Muslim Talaq (husband-initiated): Written notice to the Union Parishad/City Corporation Chairman, followed by 90-day reconciliation period.
  • Khula (wife-initiated): Wife returns mahr (dower) and applies through Chairman or family court.
  • Judicial divorce (Faskh): Court-ordered divorce on grounds such as cruelty, desertion, or non-maintenance — for Muslim, Hindu, or Christian spouses.
  • Mutual consent divorce: Both parties agree, joint notice to Chairman, 90-day waiting period.

A divorce lawyer in Uttara will identify which route applies and prepare the correct filings.

sections.h2: Documents Required to File for Divorce
sections.content:
Gather these documents before visiting the family court:

  • Original Kabinnama (marriage registration certificate) and certified copy
  • National ID cards of both parties
  • Birth certificates of children (if applicable)
  • Notice copy sent to Union Parishad Chairman (for Muslim divorce)
  • Chairman's acknowledgment receipt
  • Any evidence supporting grounds (for contested divorce): medical reports, witness statements, police records
  • Two passport photos

sections.h2: Filing in Family Court: Step by Step
sections.content:
For a judicial divorce through family court:

  1. Engage a divorce lawyer to draft the plaint (petition).
  2. The plaint must state: parties' names, marriage details, grounds for divorce, and relief sought (divorce, maintenance, custody).
  3. File the plaint at the Family Court in the jurisdiction where the wife resides or where the marriage took place.
  4. Pay the court fee (stamp duty on the plaint).
  5. The court issues summons to the other party.
  6. Both parties attend initial hearing. Mediation is attempted first.
  7. If mediation fails, trial proceeds with evidence and witnesses.
  8. Court passes decree of divorce.

Court costs for a divorce petition in Bangladesh include:

  • Court fee/stamp duty on plaint: Typically BDT 200–500 depending on relief sought.
  • Process fee for serving summons: BDT 50–200 per party.
  • Lawyer fees: Varies by seniority and case complexity — discuss upfront.
  • Miscellaneous: Certified copies of documents, affidavit stamps.

A contested divorce is significantly more expensive than a mutual consent divorce due to prolonged hearings.

sections.h2: Timeline: How Long Does Divorce Take?
sections.content:
Expected timeframes:

  • Muslim talaq/khula (uncontested): 90 days from Chairman's notice + registration time ≈ 3–4 months total.
  • Mutual consent divorce: 3–4 months.
  • Contested judicial divorce: 1–3 years depending on court load and cooperation of parties.

Engaging an experienced family court lawyer helps avoid procedural delays that extend cases unnecessarily.

A divorce lawyer in Bangladesh handles:

  • Preparing and filing the plaint with all required documents
  • Drafting the Chairman notice (for Muslim divorce)
  • Representing you at all court hearings
  • Negotiating custody, maintenance, and mahr settlements
  • Obtaining and registering the divorce certificate

Advocate Md. Shah Alam in Uttara, Dhaka handles all family court matters with sensitivity and expertise. Contact us for a confidential consultation.

faqs.question: Can I file for divorce without going to court in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Muslim talaq and uncontested khula can be completed through the Union Parishad/City Corporation without a court case, provided both parties cooperate. A lawyer is still needed to draft the notice and register the divorce.

faqs.question: What if my spouse refuses to sign divorce documents?
faqs.answer: You can still file a judicial divorce petition in family court on valid grounds. The court can proceed even if the other party is uncooperative or absent.

faqs.question: Can I claim maintenance while the divorce case is pending?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can apply for interim maintenance (nafaqa) simultaneously with the divorce petition. The court can order the husband to pay maintenance during the pendency of the case.

faqs.question: Where should I file my divorce case in Dhaka?
faqs.answer: File in the Family Court of the jurisdiction (thana) where the wife ordinarily resides, or where the marriage was solemnised. For Uttara, file at the relevant family court in Dhaka.

faqs.question: Is a divorce certificate required after the court decree?
faqs.answer: Yes. The divorce must be registered with the local Union Parishad or City Corporation to obtain an official divorce certificate, which is needed for re-marriage, inheritance, and official records.
